titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Antoine Spennato Collection of Jacques Spacagna
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday November 10, 2025 at 23:17

did.unittitle: Antoine Spennato collection of Jacques Spacagna

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: 10 boxes + 1 art, 1 roll, 1 record album storage
did.abstract: The collection contains artwork, correspondence, photographs, exhibition catalogs, fliers, posters, computer media, sound recordings, and other papers by or relating to French artist and poet Jacques Spacagna and others involved with the Lettrist movement.

bioghist.head: Jacques Spacagna (1936-1990)
bioghist.p: Jacques Spacagna was a visual and sound poet and painter who joined the Lettrist movement in the early 1960s. Along with Roberto Altman, Roland Sabatier, Alain Satié, and Micheline Hachette, Spacagna belonged to the second generation Lettrists who revived the movement after its fragmentation into rival factions at the end of the 1950s. A major contributor to Ur , Spacagna also worked with visual and sound poets beyond Lettrist circles, most notably Henri Chopin, who featured his work in Ou , Bernard Heidsieck, and Pierre Albert. Although he formally left the movement in 1972, Spacagna continued to contribute to Lettrist exhibitions and publishing projects until his death in 1992.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged roughly in the order received, with materials grouped together by format.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains artwork, correspondence, photographs, exhibition catalogs, fliers, posters, computer media, sound recordings, and other papers by or relating to French artist and poet Jacques Spacagna and others involved with the Lettrist movement. Artwork consists of Lettrist visual poetry by Spacagna, including ink and pencil drawings, collages, etchings, paintings, hypergraphy, lithographs, and screen prints. The collection also contains artwork by Gérard-Philippe Broutin, Isidore Isou, and Aude Jessemin. Sound recordings include sound art by composer Frédéric Acquaviva.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Antoine Spennato on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

Box 13 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 14 (long-playing records): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Antoine Spennato Collection of Jacques Spacagna. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Antoine Spennato Collection of Jacques Spacagna is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Printed material received with the collection was removed for separate cataloging and can be accessed by searching the library's online catalog.

did.unittitle: Broutin, Gérard-Philippe. "Suivant le rythme," ink and gouache drawing
De Charmoy, Cozette. Untitled, offset lithograph, signed
Hubaut, Joël. "Tripes à la mode de Caen," offset lithograph, signed
"Hypergraphie Polylogue," screen print on canvas, signed
Serge III. "Les enfants du paradis," mixed media on panel
Untitled, color pencil on paper, signed
"Autres rives, paysage IV," ink drawing, signed
"A toi d'jouer," print on carbon paper
Print on portfolio cover from UR 2
"Fragments d'ecrits d' UR ," ink and watercolor drawing, signed
"Hypergraphie (OU)," metallic paint on card stock, signed
"Hypergraphie pour Alain Bosquet," ink drawing, signed
Ink drawing on card for Robert Altmann, signed
"Soulèvement de la jeunesse," offset lithograph and ink drawing, signed
"Prélude à la renaissance hypergraphique," etching, signed
"Pour ainsi dire," etching in blue, signed
"Pour ainsi dire," etching in yellow, signed
"Meilleur vœux pour 1973," ink and paint on paper, signed
"L'herbier des rues," ink and pencil drawing, signed
"L'hypergraphe géomètre," ink and paint on paper, signed
"L'hypergraphe aux oripeaux," ink and paint on paper, signed
"La mère, l'enfant, les quatres jeudi," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Les poupées hypergraphiques," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Hypergraphes et leur orgue de barbarie," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Saltimbanques hypergraphes," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Le tuteur hypergraphe," ink and paint on paper, signed
"La fée hypergraphe," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Le sage (hypergraphie)," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Le souvenir du mort," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Le gardien des formes (hypergraphie)," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Fragment d'une fresque pour les hypergraphes," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Hypergraphe de la grande nébuleuse," ink and paint on paper, signed
"L'oiseau hypergraphe," ink and paint on paper, signed
"L'imperturbable (hypergraphie)," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Le mutisme (hypergraphie)," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Les moissons à Dourdan (hypergraphie)," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Pape d'un grenier hypergraphique," ink and paint on paper, signed
"Pourquoi Vérone existe-t-il au mois de mai," screen print on canvas, signed
Bertini, Gianni. "6eme Festival International de Musique Expérimentale de Bourges," screen print
Heidsieck, Bernard. "8eme Festival International de Musique Expérimentale de Bourges," screen print

did.unittitle: Correspondence from Jacques Spacagna to Paula Lescarret

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 13 (electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: See Boxes 1 and 4 for the album artwork associated with certain computer media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 13 (long-playing records): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated Materials: See Box 4 for the album artwork associated with each sound recording.
